NKJV.1: Now on the first day of the week Mary Magdalene went to the tomb early, while it was still dark, and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
NKJV.2: Then she ran and came to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid Him.”
NKJV.3: Peter therefore went out, and the other disciple, and were going to the tomb.
NKJV.4: So they both ran together, and the other disciple outran Peter and came to the tomb first.
NKJV.5: And he, stooping down and looking in, saw the linen cloths lying there; yet he did not go in.
NKJV.6: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and went into the tomb; and he saw the linen cloths lying there,
NKJV.7: and the handkerchief that had been around His head, not lying with the linen cloths, but folded together in a place by itself.
NKJV.8: Then the other disciple, who came to the tomb first, went in also; and he saw and believed.
NKJV.9: For as yet they did not know the Scripture, that He must rise again from the dead.
NKJV.10: Then the disciples went away again to their own homes.
NKJV.11: But Mary stood outside by the tomb weeping, and as she wept she stooped down and looked into the tomb.
NKJV.12: And she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
NKJV.13: Then they said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid Him.”
NKJV.14: Now when she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, and did not know that it was Jesus.
NKJV.15: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?” She, supposing Him to be the gardener, said to Him, “Sir, if You have carried Him away, tell me where You have laid Him, and I will take Him away.”
NKJV.16: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to Him, “Rabboni!” (which is to say, Teacher).
NKJV.17: Jesus said to her, “Do not cling to Me, for I have not yet ascended to My Father; but go to My brethren and say to them, ‘I am ascending to My Father and your Father, and to My God and your God.’ ”
NKJV.18: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that He had spoken these things to her.
NKJV.19: Then, the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst, and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
NKJV.20: When He had said this, He showed them His hands and His side. Then the disciples were glad when they saw the Lord.
NKJV.21: So Jesus said to them again, “Peace to you!  As the Father has sent Me, I also send you.”
NKJV.22: And when He had said this, He breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.23: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you retain the sins of any, they are retained.”
NKJV.24: Now Thomas, called the Twin, one of the twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
NKJV.25: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord.” So he said to them, “Unless I see in His hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into His side, I will not believe.”
NKJV.26: And after eight days His disciples were again inside, and Thomas with them. Jesus came, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, “Peace to you!”
NKJV.27: Then He said to Thomas, “Reach your finger here, and look at My hands; and reach your hand here, and put it into My side. Do not be unbelieving, but believing.”
NKJV.28: And Thomas answered and said to Him, “My Lord and my God!”
NKJV.29: Jesus said to him, “Thomas, because you have seen Me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”
NKJV.30: And truly Jesus did many other signs in the presence of His disciples, which are not written in this book;
NKJV.31: but these are written that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in His name.

ESV.1: Now on the first day of the week Mary Magdalene came to the tomb early, while it was still dark, and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
ESV.2: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.”
ESV.3: So Peter went out with the other disciple, and they were going toward the tomb.
ESV.4: Both of them were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
ESV.5: And stooping to look in, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he did not go in.
ESV.6: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and went into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying there,
ESV.7: and the face cloth, which had been on Jesus'  head, not lying with the linen cloths but folded up in a place by itself.
ESV.8: Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and believed;
ESV.9: for as yet they did not understand the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.
ESV.10: Then the disciples went back to their homes.
ESV.11: But Mary stood weeping outside the tomb, and as she wept she stooped to look into the tomb.
ESV.12: And she saw two angels in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had lain, one at the head and one at the feet.
ESV.13: They said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “They have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid him.”
ESV.14: Having said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
ESV.15: Jesus said to her,  “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?” Supposing him to be the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
ESV.16: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to him in Aramaic,  “Rabboni!” (which means Teacher).
ESV.17: Jesus said to her, “Do not cling to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father; but go to my brothers and say to them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
ESV.18: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord” — and that he had said these things to her.
ESV.19: On the evening of that day, the first day of the week, the doors being locked where the disciples were for fear of the Jews,  Jesus came and stood among them and said to them,  “Peace be with you.”
ESV.20: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples were glad when they saw the Lord.
ESV.21: Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, even so I am sending you.”
ESV.22: And when he had said this, he breathed on them and said to them,  “Receive the Holy Spirit.
ESV.23: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you withhold forgiveness from any, it is withheld.”
ESV.24: Now Thomas, one of the twelve, called the Twin,  was not with them when Jesus came.
ESV.25: So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord.” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the mark of the nails, and place my finger into the mark of the nails, and place my hand into his side, I will never believe.”
ESV.26: Eight days later, his disciples were inside again, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said,  “Peace be with you.”
ESV.27: Then he said to Thomas,  “Put your finger here, and see my hands; and put out your hand, and place it in my side. Do not disbelieve, but believe.”
ESV.28: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”
ESV.29: Jesus said to him, “Have you believed because you have seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”
ESV.30: Now Jesus did many other signs in the presence of the disciples, which are not written in this book;
ESV.31: but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.

NASB.1: Now on the first [day] of the week Mary Magdalene came early to the tomb, while it was still dark, and saw the stone [already] taken away from the tomb.
NASB.2: So she ran and came to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, 'They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid Him.'
NASB.3: So Peter and the other disciple went forth, and they were going to the tomb.
NASB.4: The two were running together; and the other disciple ran ahead faster than Peter and came to the tomb first;
NASB.5: and stooping and looking in, he saw the linen wrappings lying [there]; but he did not go in.
NASB.6: And so Simon Peter also came, following him, and entered the tomb; and he saw the linen wrappings lying [there],
NASB.7: and the face-cloth which had been on His head, not lying with the linen wrappings, but rolled up in a place by itself.
NASB.8: So the other disciple who had first come to the tomb then also entered, and he saw and believed.
NASB.9: For as yet they did not understand the Scripture, that He must rise again from the dead.
NASB.10: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.
NASB.11: But Mary was standing outside the tomb weeping; and so, as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb;
NASB.12: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had been lying.
NASB.13: And they said to her, 'Woman, why are you weeping?' She said to them, 'Because they have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid Him.'
NASB.14: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing [there], and did not know that it was Jesus.
NASB.15: Jesus said to her, 'Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?' Supposing Him to be the gardener, she said to Him, 'Sir, if you have carried Him away, tell me where you have laid Him, and I will take Him away.'
NASB.16: Jesus said to her, 'Mary!' She turned and said to Him in Hebrew, 'Rabboni!' (which means, Teacher).
NASB.17: Jesus said to her, 'Stop clinging to Me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father; but go to My brethren and say to them, 'I ascend to My Father and your Father, and My God and your God.''
NASB.18: Mary Magdalene came, announcing to the disciples, 'I have seen the Lord,' and [that] He had said these things to her.
NASB.19: So when it was evening on that day, the first [day] of the week, and when the doors were shut where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in their midst and said to them, 'Peace [be] with you.'
NASB.20: And when He had said this, He showed them both His hands and His side. The disciples then rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
NASB.21: So Jesus said to them again, 'Peace [be] with you; as the Father has sent Me, I also send you.'
NASB.22: And when He had said this, He breathed on them and said to them, 'Receive the Holy Spirit.
NASB.23: 'If you forgive the sins of any, [their sins] have been forgiven them; if you retain the [sins] of any, they have been retained.'
NASB.24: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
NASB.25: So the other disciples were saying to him, 'We have seen the Lord!' But he said to them, 'Unless I see in His hands the imprint of the nails, and put my finger into the place of the nails, and put my hand into His side, I will not believe.'
NASB.26: After eight days His disciples were again inside, and Thomas with them. Jesus came, the doors having been shut, and stood in their midst and said, 'Peace [be] with you.'
NASB.27: Then He said to Thomas, 'Reach here with your finger, and see My hands; and reach here your hand and put it into My side; and do not be unbelieving, but believing.'
NASB.28: Thomas answered and said to Him, 'My Lord and my God!'
NASB.29: Jesus said to him, 'Because you have seen Me, have you believed? Blessed [are] they who did not see, and [yet] believed.'
NASB.30: Therefore many other signs Jesus also performed in the presence of the disciples, which are not written in this book;
NASB.31: but these have been written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing you may have life in His name.

NLT.1: Early on Sunday morning, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and found that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
NLT.2: She ran and found Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved. She said, “They have taken the Lord’s body out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have put him!”
NLT.3: Peter and the other disciple started out for the tomb.
NLT.4: They were both running, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
NLT.5: He stooped and looked in and saw the linen wrappings lying there, but he didn’t go in.
NLT.6: Then Simon Peter arrived and went inside. He also noticed the linen wrappings lying there,
NLT.7: while the cloth that had covered Jesus’ head was folded up and lying apart from the other wrappings.
NLT.8: Then the disciple who had reached the tomb first also went in, and he saw and believed —
NLT.9: for until then they still hadn’t understood the Scriptures that said Jesus must rise from the dead.
NLT.11: Mary was standing outside the tomb crying, and as she wept, she stooped and looked in.
NLT.12: She saw two white-robed angels, one sitting at the head and the other at the foot of the place where the body of Jesus had been lying.
NLT.13: “Dear woman, why are you crying?” the angels asked her. “Because they have taken away my Lord,” she replied, “and I don’t know where they have put him.”
NLT.14: She turned to leave and saw someone standing there. It was Jesus, but she didn’t recognize him.
NLT.15: “Dear woman, why are you crying?” Jesus asked her. “Who are you looking for?” She thought he was the gardener. “Sir,” she said, “if you have taken him away, tell me where you have put him, and I will go and get him.”
NLT.16: “Mary!” Jesus said. She turned to him and cried out, “Rabboni!” (which is Hebrew for “Teacher”).
NLT.17: “Don’t cling to me,” Jesus said, “for I haven’t yet ascended to the Father. But go find my brothers and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
NLT.18: Mary Magdalene found the disciples and told them, “I have seen the Lord!” Then she gave them his message.
NLT.19: That Sunday evening the disciples were meeting behind locked doors because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders. Suddenly, Jesus was standing there among them! “Peace be with you,” he said.
NLT.20: As he spoke, he showed them the wounds in his hands and his side. They were filled with joy when they saw the Lord!
NLT.21: Again he said, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, so I am sending you.”
NLT.22: Then he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
NLT.23: If you forgive anyone’s sins, they are forgiven. If you do not forgive them, they are not forgiven.”
NLT.24: One of the twelve disciples, Thomas (nicknamed the Twin), was not with the others when Jesus came.
NLT.25: They told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he replied, “I won’t believe it unless I see the nail wounds in his hands, put my fingers into them, and place my hand into the wound in his side.”
NLT.26: Eight days later the disciples were together again, and this time Thomas was with them. The doors were locked; but suddenly, as before, Jesus was standing among them. “Peace be with you,” he said.
NLT.27: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here, and look at my hands. Put your hand into the wound in my side. Don’t be faithless any longer. Believe!”
NLT.28: “My Lord and my God!” Thomas exclaimed.
NLT.29: Then Jesus told him, “You believe because you have seen me. Blessed are those who believe without seeing me.”
NLT.30: The disciples saw Jesus do many other miraculous signs in addition to the ones recorded in this book.
NLT.31: But these are written so that you may continue to believe that Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God, and that by believing in him you will have life by the power of his name.

WEB.1: Now on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went early, while it was still dark, to the tomb, and saw the stone taken away from the tomb.
WEB.2: Therefore she ran and came to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have laid him!”
WEB.3: Therefore Peter and the other disciple went out, and they went toward the tomb.
WEB.4: They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter, and came to the tomb first.
WEB.5: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying, yet he didn’t enter in.
WEB.6: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and entered into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying,
WEB.7: and the cloth that had been on his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in a place by itself.
WEB.8: So then the other disciple who came first to the tomb also entered in, and he saw and believed.
WEB.9: For as yet they didn’t know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.
WEB.10: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.
WEB.11: But Mary was standing outside at the tomb weeping. So, as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb,
WEB.12: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
WEB.13: They told her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don’t know where they have laid him.”
WEB.14: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, and didn’t know that it was Jesus.
WEB.15: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?” She, supposing him to be the gardener, said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
WEB.16: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to him, “Rabboni!”  which is to say, “Teacher!”
WEB.17: Jesus said to her, “Don’t hold me, for I haven’t yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers, and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
WEB.18: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had said these things to her.
WEB.19: When therefore it was evening, on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were locked where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the middle, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”
WEB.20: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad when they saw the Lord.
WEB.21: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Peace be to you. As the Father has sent me, even so I send you.”
WEB.22: When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit!
WEB.23: If you forgive anyone’s sins, they have been forgiven them. If you retain anyone’s sins, they have been retained.”
WEB.24: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, wasn’t with them when Jesus came.
WEB.25: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.”
WEB.26: After eight days again his disciples were inside, and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, the doors being locked, and stood in the middle, and said, “Peace be to you.”
WEB.27: Then he said to Thomas, “Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don’t be unbelieving, but believing.”
WEB.28: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”
WEB.29: Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen me,  you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed.”
WEB.30: Therefore Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book;
WEB.31: but these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.

CSB17.1: On <sup>ⓖ</sup> the first day of the week <sup>ⓗ</sup> Mary Magdalene <sup>ⓘ</sup> came to the tomb <sup>ⓙ</sup> early, while it was still dark. She saw that the stone <sup>ⓚ</sup> had been removed from the tomb.
CSB17.2: So she went running to Simon Peter <sup>ⓛ</sup> and to the other disciple, the one Jesus loved, and said to them, “They’ve taken the Lord out of the tomb, <sup>ⓜ</sup> and we don’t know where they’ve put him!”
CSB17.3: At that, Peter and the other disciple went out, heading for the tomb.
CSB17.4: The two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and got to the tomb first.
CSB17.5: Stooping down, he saw the linen cloths <sup>ⓝ</sup> lying there, but he did not go in.
CSB17.6: Then, following him, Simon Peter also came. He entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
CSB17.7: The wrapping <sup>ⓐ</sup> that had been on his head was not lying with the linen cloths <sup>ⓑ</sup> but was folded up in a separate place by itself.
CSB17.8: The other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, then also went in, saw, and believed.
CSB17.9: For they did not yet understand the Scripture <sup>ⓒ</sup> that he must rise <sup>ⓓ</sup> from the dead. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.10: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
CSB17.11: But <sup>ⓕ</sup> Mary stood outside the tomb, <sup>ⓖ</sup> crying. As she was crying, she stooped to look into the tomb.
CSB17.12: She saw two angels <sup>ⓗ</sup> in white sitting where Jesus’s body <sup>ⓘ</sup> had been lying, one at the head and the other at the feet.
CSB17.13: They said to her, “Woman, why are you crying?” “Because they’ve taken away my Lord,” <sup>ⓙ</sup> she told them, “and I don’t know where they’ve put him.”
CSB17.14: Having said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know it was Jesus. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.15: “Woman,” Jesus said to her, “why are you crying? Who is it that you’re seeking?” Supposing he was the gardener, she replied, “Sir, if you’ve carried him away, tell me where you’ve put him, and I will take him away.”
CSB17.16: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” Turning around, she said to him in Aramaic, [1] “Rabboni!”  — which means “Teacher.” ⓛ
CSB17.17: “Don’t cling to me,” Jesus told her, “since I have not yet ascended <sup>ⓜ</sup>to the Father. <sup>ⓝ</sup>But go to my brothers <sup>ⓐ</sup>and tell them that I am ascending to my Father <sup>ⓑ</sup> and your Father, to my God <sup>ⓒ</sup>and your God.”
CSB17.18: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord!” <sup>ⓓ</sup> And she told them what <sup>[2]</sup> he had said to her.
CSB17.19: When it was evening of that first day of the week, <sup>ⓔ</sup> the disciples were gathered together with the doors locked because they feared the Jews. Jesus came, stood among them, and said to them, “Peace be with you.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.20: Having said this, he showed them his hands and his side. <sup>ⓖ</sup> So the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
CSB17.21: Jesus said to them again, “Peace to you. As the Father has sent me, <sup>ⓗ</sup> I also send you.” <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.22: After saying this, he breathed on them <sup>ⓙ</sup> and said, <sup>[3]</sup> “Receive the Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.23: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you retain the sins of any, they are retained.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: But Thomas <sup>ⓜ</sup> (called “Twin” <sup>[4]</sup>), one of the Twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
CSB17.25: So the other disciples were telling him, “We’ve seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “If I don’t see the mark of the nails in his hands, put my finger into the mark of the nails, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and put my hand into his side, <sup>ⓐ</sup> I will never believe.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.26: A week later his disciples were indoors again, and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
CSB17.27: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands. Reach out your hand and put it into my side. Don’t be faithless, but believe.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.28: Thomas responded to him, “My Lord and my God!”
CSB17.29: Jesus said, “Because you have seen me, you have believed. <sup>[5]</sup>Blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.30: Jesus performed many other signs <sup>ⓔ</sup> in the presence of his disciples that are not written <sup>ⓕ</sup> in this book. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.31: But these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Messiah, <sup>ⓗ</sup> the Son <sup>ⓘ</sup> of God, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> and that by believing you may have life in his name. <sup>ⓚ</sup>

AMP.1: Now on the first day of the week Mary Magdalene came to the tomb early, while it was still dark, and saw the stone [already] removed from the [groove across the entrance of the] tomb.
AMP.2: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and to the other disciple (John), whom Jesus loved (esteemed), and said to them, "They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid Him!"
AMP.3: So Peter and the other disciple left, and they were going to the tomb.
AMP.4: And the two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and arrived at the tomb first.
AMP.5: Stooping down and looking in, he saw the linen wrappings [neatly] lying there; but he did not go in.
AMP.6: Then Simon Peter came up, following him, and went into the tomb and saw the linen wrappings [neatly] lying there;
AMP.7: and the [burial] face-cloth which had been on Jesus' head, not lying with the [other] linen wrappings, but rolled up in a place by itself.
AMP.8: So the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, went in too; and he saw [the wrappings and the face-cloth] and believed [without any doubt that Jesus had risen from the dead].
AMP.9: For as yet they did not understand the Scripture, that He must rise from the dead.
AMP.10: Then the disciples went back again to their own homes.
AMP.11: But Mary [who had returned] was standing outside the tomb sobbing; and so, as she wept, she stooped down and looked into the tomb;
AMP.12: and she saw two angels in white sitting there, one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
AMP.13: And they said to her, "Woman, why are you crying?" She told them, "Because they have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid Him."
AMP.14: After saying this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
AMP.15: Jesus said to her, "Woman, why are you crying? For whom are you looking?" Supposing that He was the gardener, she replied, "Sir, if you are the one who has carried Him away from here, tell me where you have put Him, and I will take Him away."
AMP.16: Jesus said to her, "Mary!" She turned and said to Him in Hebrew, "Rabboni!" (which means, Teacher).
AMP.17: Jesus said to her, " Do not hold Me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father; but go to My brothers and tell them, 'I am ascending to My Father and your Father, and to My God and your God.'"
AMP.18: Mary Magdalene came, reporting to the disciples that she had seen the Lord and that He had said these things to her.
AMP.19: So when it was evening on that same day, the first day of the week, though the disciples were [meeting] behind barred doors for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them, and said, " Peace to you."
AMP.20: After He said this, He showed them His hands and His side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with great joy.
AMP.21: Then Jesus said to them again, "Peace to you; as the Father has sent Me, I also send you [as My representatives]."
AMP.22: And when He said this, He breathed on them and said to them, "Receive the Holy Spirit.
AMP.23: If you forgive the sins of anyone they are forgiven [because of their faith]; if you retain the sins of anyone, they are retained [and remain unforgiven because of their unbelief]."
AMP.24: But Thomas, one of the twelve [disciples], who was called Didymus (the twin), was not with them when Jesus came.
AMP.25: So the other disciples kept telling him, "We have seen the Lord!" But he said to them, "Unless I see in His hands the marks of the nails, and put my finger into the nail prints, and put my hand into His side, I will never believe."
AMP.26: Eight days later His disciples were again inside the house, and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, though the doors had been barred, and stood among them and said, "Peace to you."
AMP.27: Then He said to Thomas, "Reach here with your finger, and see My hands; and put out your hand and place it in My side. Do not be unbelieving, but [stop doubting and] believe."
AMP.28: Thomas answered Him, "My Lord and my God!"
AMP.29: Jesus said to him, "Because you have seen Me, do you now believe? Blessed [happy, spiritually secure, and favored by God] are they who did not see [Me] and yet believed [in Me]."
AMP.30: There are also many other signs (attesting miracles) that Jesus performed in the presence of the disciples, which are not written in this book;
AMP.31: but these have been written so that you may believe [with a deep, abiding trust] that Jesus is the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), the Son of God; and that by believing [and trusting in and relying on Him] you may have life in His name.

MSG.1: Early in the morning on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone was moved away from the entrance.
MSG.2: She ran at once to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one Jesus loved, breathlessly panting, "They took the Master from the tomb. We don't know where they've put him."
MSG.3: Peter and the other disciple left immediately for the tomb.
MSG.4: They ran, neck and neck. The other disciple got to the tomb first, outrunning Peter.
MSG.5: Stooping to look in, he saw the pieces of linen cloth lying there, but he didn't go in.
MSG.6: Simon Peter arrived after him, entered the tomb, observed the linen cloths lying there,
MSG.7: and the kerchief used to cover his head not lying with the linen cloths but separate, neatly folded by itself.
MSG.8: Then the other disciple, the one who had gotten there first, went into the tomb, took one look at the evidence, and believed.
MSG.9: No one yet knew from the Scripture that he had to rise from the dead.
MSG.10: The disciples then went back home.
MSG.11: But Mary stood outside the tomb weeping. As she wept, she knelt to look into the tomb
MSG.12: and saw two angels sitting there, dressed in white, one at the head, the other at the foot of where Jesus' body had been laid.
MSG.13: They said to her, "Woman, why do you weep?" "They took my Master," she said, "and I don't know where they put him."
MSG.14: After she said this, she turned away and saw Jesus standing there. But she didn't recognize him.
MSG.15: Jesus spoke to her, "Woman, why do you weep? Who are you looking for?" She, thinking that he was the gardener, said, "Mister, if you took him, tell me where you put him so I can care for him."
MSG.16: Jesus said, "Mary." Turning to face him, she said in Hebrew, "Rabboni!" meaning "Teacher!"
MSG.17: Jesus said, "Don't cling to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. Go to my brothers and tell them, 'I ascend to my Father and your Father, my God and your God.'"
MSG.18: Mary Magdalene went, telling the news to the disciples: "I saw the Master!" And she told them everything he said to her.
MSG.19: Later on that day, the disciples had gathered together, but, fearful of the Jews, had locked all the doors in the house. Jesus entered, stood among them, and said, "Peace to you."
MSG.20: Then he showed them his hands and side. The disciples, seeing the Master with their own eyes, were exuberant.
MSG.21: Jesus repeated his greeting: "Peace to you. Just as the Father sent me, I send you."
MSG.22: Then he took a deep breath and breathed into them. "Receive the Holy Spirit," he said.
MSG.23: "If you forgive someone's sins, they're gone for good. If you don't forgive sins, what are you going to do with them?"
MSG.24: But Thomas, sometimes called the Twin, one of the Twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
MSG.25: The other disciples told him, "We saw the Master." But he said, "Unless I see the nail holes in his hands, put my finger in the nail holes, and stick my hand in his side, I won't believe it."
MSG.26: Eight days later, his disciples were again in the room. This time Thomas was with them. Jesus came through the locked doors, stood among them, and said, "Peace to you."
MSG.27: Then he focused his attention on Thomas. "Take your finger and examine my hands. Take your hand and stick it in my side. Don't be unbelieving. Believe."
MSG.29: Jesus said, "So, you believe because you've seen with your own eyes. Even better blessings are in store for those who believe without seeing."
MSG.30: Jesus provided far more God-revealing signs than are written down in this book.
MSG.31: These are written down so you will believe that Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God, and in the act of believing, have real and eternal life in the way he personally revealed it.

NIV.1:
The Empty Tomb
Early on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been removed from the entrance.
NIV.2: So she came running to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one Jesus loved, and said, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have put him!”
NIV.3: So Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb.
NIV.4: Both were running, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
NIV.5: He bent over and looked in at the strips of linen lying there but did not go in.
NIV.6: Then Simon Peter, who was behind him, arrived and went into the tomb. He saw the strips of linen lying there,
NIV.7: as well as the burial cloth that had been around Jesus’ head. The cloth was folded up by itself, separate from the linen.
NIV.8: Finally the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
NIV.9: (They still did not understand from Scripture that Jesus had to rise from the dead.)
NIV.10:
Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene
Then the disciples went back to their homes,
NIV.11: but Mary stood outside the tomb crying. As she wept, she bent over to look into the tomb
NIV.12: and saw two angels in white, seated where Jesus’ body had been, one at the head and the other at the foot.
NIV.13:
They asked her, “Woman, why are you crying?”
“They have taken my Lord away,” she said, “and I don’t know where they have put him.”
NIV.14: At this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not realize that it was Jesus.
NIV.15:
“Woman,” he said, “why are you crying? Who is it you are looking for?”
Thinking he was the gardener, she said, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him, and I will get him.”
NIV.16:
Jesus said to her, “Mary.”
She turned toward him and cried out in Aramaic, “Rabboni!” (which means Teacher).
NIV.17: Jesus said, “Do not hold on to me, for I have not yet returned to the Father. Go instead to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am returning to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
NIV.18: Mary Magdalene went to the disciples with the news: “I have seen the Lord!” And she told them that he had said these things to her.
NIV.19:
Jesus Appears to His Disciples
On the evening of that first day of the week, when the disciples were together, with the doors locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you!”
NIV.20: After he said this, he showed them his hands and side. The disciples were overjoyed when they saw the Lord.
NIV.21: Again Jesus said, “Peace be with you! As the Father has sent me, I am sending you.”
NIV.22: And with that he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
NIV.23: If you forgive anyone his sins, they are forgiven; if you do not forgive them, they are not forgiven.”
NIV.24:
Jesus Appears to Thomas
Now Thomas (called Didymus), one of the Twelve, was not with the disciples when Jesus came.
NIV.25:
So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord!”
But he said to them, “Unless I see the nail marks in his hands and put my finger where the nails were, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe it.”
NIV.26: A week later his disciples were in the house again, and Thomas was with them. Though the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you!”
NIV.27: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here; see my hands. Reach out your hand and put it into my side. Stop doubting and believe.”
NIV.28: Thomas said to him, “My Lord and my God!”
NIV.29: Then Jesus told him, “Because you have seen me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”
NIV.30: Jesus did many other miraculous signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not recorded in this book.
NIV.31: But these are written that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.

NRSVCE.1: Early on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been removed from the tomb.
NRSVCE.2: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.”
NRSVCE.3: Then Peter and the other disciple set out and went toward the tomb.
NRSVCE.4: The two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
NRSVCE.5: He bent down to look in and saw the linen wrappings lying there, but he did not go in.
NRSVCE.6: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and went into the tomb. He saw the linen wrappings lying there,
NRSVCE.7: and the cloth that had been on Jesus’ head, not lying with the linen wrappings but rolled up in a place by itself.
NRSVCE.8: Then the other disciple, who reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and believed;
NRSVCE.9: for as yet they did not understand the scripture, that he must rise from the dead.
NRSVCE.10: Then the disciples returned to their homes.
NRSVCE.11: But Mary stood weeping outside the tomb. As she wept, she bent over to look into the tomb;
NRSVCE.12: and she saw two angels in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had been lying, one at the head and the other at the feet.
NRSVCE.13: They said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “They have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid him.”
NRSVCE.14: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
NRSVCE.15: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?” Supposing him to be the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
NRSVCE.16: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to him in Hebrew, “Rabbouni!” (which means Teacher).
NRSVCE.17: Jesus said to her, “Do not hold on to me, because I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and say to them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
NRSVCE.18: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord”; and she told them that he had said these things to her.
NRSVCE.19: When it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and the doors of the house where the disciples had met were locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
NRSVCE.20: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
NRSVCE.21: Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, so I send you.”
NRSVCE.22: When he had said this, he breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.23: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you retain the sins of any, they are retained.”
NRSVCE.24: But Thomas (who was called the Twin ), one of the twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
NRSVCE.25: So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord.” But he said to them, “Unless I see the mark of the nails in his hands, and put my finger in the mark of the nails and my hand in his side, I will not believe.”
NRSVCE.26: A week later his disciples were again in the house, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were shut, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
NRSVCE.27: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and see my hands. Reach out your hand and put it in my side. Do not doubt but believe.”
NRSVCE.28: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”
NRSVCE.29: Jesus said to him, “Have you believed because you have seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have come to believe.”
NRSVCE.30: Now Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book.
NRSVCE.31: But these are written so that you may come to believe that Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God, and that through believing you may have life in his name.

NET.1: Now very early on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been moved away from the entrance.
NET.2: So she went running to Simon Peter and the other disciple whom Jesus loved and told them, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we don’t know where they have put him!”
NET.3: Then Peter and the other disciple set out to go to the tomb.
NET.4: The two were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and reached the tomb first.
NET.5: He bent down and saw the strips of linen cloth lying there, but he did not go in.
NET.6: Then Simon Peter, who had been following him, arrived and went right into the tomb. He saw the strips of linen cloth lying there,
NET.7: and the face cloth, which had been around Jesus’ head, not lying with the strips of linen cloth but rolled up in a place by itself.
NET.8: Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, came in, and he saw and believed.
NET.9: (For they did not yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.)
NET.10: So the disciples went back to their homes.
NET.11: But Mary stood outside the tomb weeping. As she wept, she bent down and looked into the tomb.
NET.12: And she saw two angels in white sitting where Jesus’ body had been lying, one at the head and one at the feet.
NET.13: They said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” Mary replied, “They have taken my Lord away, and I do not know where they have put him!”
NET.14: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
NET.15: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?” Because she thought he was the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him, and I will take him.”
NET.16: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to him in Aramaic, “Rabboni” (which means Teacher).
NET.17: Jesus replied, “Do not touch me, for I have not yet ascended to my Father. Go to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
NET.18: Mary Magdalene came and informed the disciples, “I have seen the Lord!” And she told them what Jesus had said to her.
NET.19: On the evening of that day, the first day of the week, the disciples had gathered together and locked the doors of the place because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders. Jesus came and stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
NET.20: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
NET.21: So Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. Just as the Father has sent me, I also send you.”
NET.22: And after he said this, he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
NET.23: If you forgive anyone’s sins, they are forgiven; if you retain anyone’s sins, they are retained.”
NET.24: Now Thomas (called Didymus), one of the twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
NET.25: The other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he replied, “Unless I see the wounds from the nails in his hands, and put my finger into the wounds from the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will never believe it!”
NET.26: Eight days later the disciples were again together in the house, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you!”
NET.27: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here, and examine my hands. Extend your hand and put it into my side. Do not continue in your unbelief, but believe.”
NET.28: Thomas replied to him, “My Lord and my God!”
NET.29: Jesus said to him, “Have you believed because you have seen me? Blessed are the people who have not seen and yet have believed.”
NET.30: Now Jesus performed many other miraculous signs in the presence of the disciples, which are not recorded in this book.
NET.31: But these are recorded so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.
